verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family protein that functions exclusively in intracellular apoptotic signaling. In healthy cells, it is cytoplasmic. Upon apoptotic stress, BAX translocates to and inserts into the mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM) to form oligomeric pores that trigger cytochrome c release. There is no evidence of plasma membrane localization, surface expression, or any mechanism driving the protein body to the outer face of the plasma membrane at baseline or under any documented cellular condition. The protein's entire functional biology occurs at intracellular organellar membranes.
